meaning: n.轻视,轻蔑,耻辱,不尊敬,[律]藐视法庭(或国会)
examples: She looked at him with contempt.
They had shown a contempt for the values she thought important.
The complainant has been exposed to public odium, scandal, and contempt.
Dozens of fans were arrested, charged with contempt for religion, and heavy metal concerts were banned.
He predicted the "strong majority" of the Democratic caucus would oppose the contempt measure.
